./docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md:196:**Integration tests: zero mocks.** No exceptions. Integration tests exercise real production implementations of every dependency in the graph — real git (`t.TempDir()` + scrubbed `GIT_*` env), real filesystem, real bead store, real coordinator, real server, real agent harness (the `script` harness below is the only approved fake for the AI-provider boundary, and it does not mock — it performs real filesystem and git operations deterministically). A test that substitutes any mock for a production component is not an integration test. It is a unit test that is lying about its coverage.
./docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md:209:**The only approved fake in the DDx test suite is the `script` harness.** It is the *AI-provider* boundary replacement, and it is not a mock — it is a deterministic alternate implementation of the `agent.Runner` interface that reads a line-based directive file and performs real filesystem + git operations against the worktree. The `virtual` harness is a replay-only fake retained for prompt-routing unit tests; it does not write files or make commits.

./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:334:1. **A direct first-class agent.** Native in-process runtime over the model providers (bragi, vidar, openrouter, anthropic). Designed to be the high-performance choice for batch noninteractive prompts.
./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:344:2. **Two-role coherence.** ddx-agent's value prop ("optimally routed one-shot noninteractive prompts") only makes sense if it owns the routing **across** harnesses, not just within in-process providers. A consumer that wants "give me the best harness/provider/model for this prompt under cost constraint X" needs one entrypoint that knows about all options. ./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:480:Two-layer routing — DDx routing across harnesses (claude/codex/opencode/agent/pi), agent routing across providers (bragi/vidar/openrouter) — is **a long-standing requirement, badly implemented today**. Before locking the interface, do an archeology pass to find every existing spec, code piece, bead, and design note that touches it. The "repair" must be complete, not greenfield.
./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:486:**B. Lift all routing to DDx.** ddx-agent becomes a mechanism (list/probe/resolve/execute). DDx ranks `(harness, provider?, model)` tuples uniformly. Single-layer routing in DDx.
./docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:490:**Decisive C-favoring argument: comparison suite.** A stated project goal is that ddx-agent's native harness should *outperform* the subprocess wrappers (claude/codex/opencode) on batch noninteractive tasks. Proving that requires a comparison suite that runs identical prompts through every harness implementation and measures latency, cost, success rate, output quality. Today that suite is split: harness invocation lives in DDx (`cli/internal/agent/compare.go`, `cli/internal/agent/benchmark.go`), routing lives in DDx, but the native agent runtime lives in ddx-agent. Under C, the comparison suite collapses into one module that can A/B test (`harness=claude` subprocess) vs (`harness=agent-native`, `provider=bragi`, `model=qwen3.6`) with the same input/output schema and metrics collection. Under A or B, the suite still has to bridge two modules and measurement parity is harder to maintain.

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:52:{"id":"corpus-368f5152b39b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-d3a4f89c","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"]}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:66:{"id":"corpus-47f5902056ab","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add project-scoped route middleware and migrate document/bead/graph routes","description":"## Scope\nAdd project-scoping middleware to the DDx server and migrate all document, bead, and doc-graph HTTP routes to canonical project-scoped paths.\n\n## Context\nThe server currently registers ~50 routes under /api/... without project scoping. FEAT-002 requires /api/projects/{project}/... as canonical. Three worker routes already use this pattern (handleProjectWorkerList, handleProjectWorkerShow, handleProjectWorkerProgress) — follow that pattern.\n\n## Implementation approach\n1. Create a projectMiddleware (or helper function) that:\n   - Extracts {project} from the URL path\n   - Resolves it via GetProjectByID() or GetProjectByPath()\n   - Injects the resolved ProjectEntry into request context\n   - Returns 404 if project not found\n2. Register all document, bead, and doc-graph routes under /api/projects/{project}/...\n3. Keep legacy /api/... routes as singleton compatibility aliases that auto-resolve to the single registered project (error if \u003e1 project registered without scoping)\n4. Add test(s) that register two projects, verify scoped routes resolve correctly, and verify legacy routes work with one project\n\n## Files to touch\n- cli/internal/server/server.go — route registration, middleware\n- cli/internal/server/handlers.go (or individual handler files) — extract project from context instead of hardcoding\n- cli/internal/server/server_test.go — new project-scoped routing tests\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n- MCP tools (separate bead)\n- GraphQL schema (already has beadsByProject, workersByProject)\n- Frontend routes","acceptance":"(1) A projectMiddleware or projectResolver extracts {project} from /api/projects/{project}/... routes and injects resolved ProjectEntry into request context. (3) Legacy unscoped routes continue to work when exactly one project is registered (singleton compatibility). That older code rejects 'type: lucebox' / 'type: vllm' from the user's live config.\n\nFailing tests captured (sample):\n\n  service_upstream-services_test.go:680 TestNewWaitsBrieflyForInvalidQuotaRefresh\n  service_upstream-services_test.go:721 TestNewStartupQuotaRefreshContinuesAfterTimeout\n  service_upstream-services_test.go:765 TestPrimaryQuotaRefreshWorkerRefreshesOnTimer\n  service_validation_test.go:15 TestExecuteRejectsUnsupportedSubprocessRoute-targetBeforeRun\n  service_validation_test.go:43 TestExecuteRejectsUnsupportedSubprocessReasoningBeforeRun\n  service_runner_dispatch_test.go:41,90,119 TestExecute_Dispatches*\n  service_profiles_test.go:13,61,112 TestServiceProfiles_*\n\nAll same root cause: agent.New() loads the live user config without test isolation.\n\n## Fix paths\n\n1. Tests that don't actually need the user's config should pass an explicit ServiceConfig stub (no upstream-services, or a known shape).\n2. Or, set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp dir in test setup so agent.New() loads an empty config.\n3. Or, add an option to agent.New that skips XDG lookup.\n\nPick whichever is most consistent with how other test-isolation patterns work in this repo.\n\n## Why P2\n\nDoesn't block normal CI (CI clean container has no user config). ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:211:{"id":"corpus-e3d55d44b044","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. No dependency on worktrunk or any external binary beyond git.","labels":["ux","execute-loop","observability","cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-9060c8b2","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:229:{"id":"corpus-f546b016a732","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Implement deterministic script runner for orchestration stress testing","description":"The execute-loop / execute-bead / land-coordinator system cannot be tested end-to-end today because every \"fake\" agent in the codebase returns a canned Result{} without actually writing files or making commits. `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go` is a prompt-response dictionary replayer — it returns canned stdout based on prompt hash but does not touch the filesystem or git. `fakeAgentRunner` and `fakeExecuteBeadGit` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go` are in-memory mocks that track expected call counts. Neither produces actual commits that the orchestration layer can rebase, merge, or land.\n\nThe consequence: every test that claims to cover concurrency, serialization, or git orchestration uses mocked git and therefore cannot catch merge-race, rebase-conflict, or coordinator-serialization bugs. `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace` at execute_bead_orchestrator_test.go:228 is particularly misleading — it uses separate orchTestGitOps instances per worker so the shared-writer invariant is never exercised. The test passes because no shared state exists to race on.\n\n## What this bead builds\n\nA new deterministic `script` runner (cli/internal/agent/script.go, mirroring cli/internal/agent/virtual.go):\n\n- Registered alongside claude/codex/agent/virtual in cli/internal/agent/registry.go\n- Always available (no binary probe required)\n- Takes a directive file (passed via RunOptions.Route-target or a new RunOptions.ScriptFile field — either works)\n- Reads the directive file, executes each line against opts.WorkDir as real filesystem + git operations\n- Returns a real Result{ExitCode, Output} that the loop and orchestrator can observe\n\n## Directive grammar\n\nLine-based, comments start with `#`. Each directive performs a real side effect:\n\n```\nappend-line \u003cpath\u003e \u003ctext\u003e          # append text to file (create if missing)\ncreate-file \u003cpath\u003e \u003ccontent\u003e       # create or overwrite file\ndelete-file \u003cpath\u003e                 # rm -f path\nmodify-line \u003cpath\u003e \u003cregex\u003e \u003crepl\u003e  # sed-style in-place substitution\nrun \u003cshell-command\u003e                # exec in WorkDir with clean env\ncommit \u003cmessage\u003e                   # git add -A \u0026\u0026 git commit -m \u003cmessage\u003e\nset-exit \u003ccode\u003e                    # final exit code (default 0)\nno-op                              # no commits, no files, exit 0\nfail-during \u003cindex\u003e                # synthetic failure at the Nth directive\nsleep-ms \u003cn\u003e                       # deterministic delay (for scheduling tests)\n```\n\nDirectives run in order. Environment variables DDX_BEAD_ID, DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, DDX_WORKER_ID are interpolated into text arguments so directives can be parameterized. Example:\n\n```\nappend-line tests/smoke.txt \"hello from ${DDX_BEAD_ID}\"\ncommit \"smoke: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}\"\n```\n\n## Invocation\n\nFrom the CLI: `ddx agent run --runner=script --route-target=./testdata/append-smoke.txt --prompt=/dev/null`\n\nProgrammatically from a test:\n```go\nrunner := agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{Runner: \"script\"})\nresult, err := runner.Run(agent.RunOptions{\n    WorkDir: wtPath,\n    Route-target:   \"testdata/append-smoke.txt\",\n    Correlation: map[string]string{\"bead_id\": beadID, \"attempt_id\": attemptID},\n})\n```\n\n## Implementation notes\n\n- Mirror the dispatch pattern in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:81 where `virtual` is intercepted\n- Always-available in Discover() same as virtual + agent\n- No external dependencies beyond what the agent package already uses (os, os/exec for git)\n- Estimated size: ~200 lines of implementation + ~200 lines of tests\n- Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go must prove each directive actually performs its claimed side effect on a real temp git repo\n\n## What this enables\n\nEvery test case in beads B (integration tests) and C (server runWorker regression test) depends on this runner existing. Without it, the land-coordinator redesign ships behind whatever real claude happens to do on the day the test runs.","acceptance":"New file cli/internal/agent/script.go implements RunScript(opts) that reads a directive file, executes directives against opts.WorkDir using real filesystem and git operations, and returns a real Result. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:238:{"id":"corpus-011259e83fe4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"execute-loop\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","bug","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-05b4cc9d","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. ./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:419:| Worker state | Meaning | Entry | Exit |
./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:421:| `idle` | Worker is up but not actively claiming. | Worker startup; transient between attempts. | Operator or scheduler moves it to `draining`. |
./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:422:| `draining` | Worker is actively claiming and executing beads. | Default operating state. | Quota or rate-limit pause; explicit stop. |
./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:423:| `paused-quota` | Worker has observed quota exhaustion on its harness. | `drain-paused-quota` event. | `drain-resumed-quota` event after backoff or operator resume. |
./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:424:| `paused-rate-limit` | Worker is sleeping out a rate-limit retry window inside an attempt. | Rate-limit response from harness. | Wait window elapses; worker resumes the same attempt. |
./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:425:| `exiting` | Worker is shutting down cleanly; will not claim more beads. | Operator stop or terminal failure. | Process exit. |

./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t        )\\\",\\n29\\t    )\\n30\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t    // Create users table.\\n33\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t            email              TEXT,\\n38\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\\n40\\t        )\\\",\\n41\\t    )\\n42\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t    // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t            PRIMARY KEY {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. ./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev test `cutover_no_auth_header_falls_through_to_no_auth_201` confirms |\\n\\n### Summary\\n\\nThe HTTP data-plane side is well-implemented: the optional JWT layer, audit attribution threading via thread-local, and the comprehensive test suite are solid. However, AC #5 (gRPC JWT verify path in `check_auth`) is completely absent from the diff — the gRPC service received only the header rename, not the JWT authentication path.\\n\\n### Findings\\n\\n- **BLOCK — gRPC JWT verify path missing**: `crates/axon-server/src/service.rs` `check_auth` was not modified to accept JWT credentials via the `authorization` metadata key. The bead description explicitly requires \\\"Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\\\" and \\\"Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\\\" No JWT-related imports, no `ResolvedIdentity`, no `JwtIssuer` reference exists in the gRPC service diff.\\n- **REQUEST_CHANGES — Thread-local attribution pattern is fragile**: `handler.rs` uses a `thread_local!` + `RefCell` to pass attribution from gateway to handler. The comment claims safety because handler writes are synchronous, but if Tokio's work-stealing runtime reschedules the task between `set_pending_attribution(Some(...))` and the handler call, a different request's attribution could leak. The pattern works today but is a latent correctness risk. ./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:59:| D5 | **Wire transport**: GraphQL only. | All DDx↔Axon traffic is GraphQL — queries and mutations over HTTP, subscriptions over WebSocket. No gRPC, no REST, no MCP from the DDx side. The Go client is generated by `genqlient` from a pinned schema-introspection snapshot. Rationale: Axon's GraphQL surface already covers entity CRUD, links, traversal, transactions, schema, and subscriptions; DDx is already a GraphQL server internally (`cli/internal/server/graphql_*.go`), so DDx↔Axon is wire-symmetric; subscriptions unlock live UI updates for `ddx work` (Story 8) without polling; no protoc/codegen toolchain enters the DDx build. The per-call wire-overhead advantage of gRPC is immeasurable at DDx scale (low thousands of beads); if batch-transaction throughput later becomes painful for one specific path, gRPC can be added there without revisiting this decision. |

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:52:{"id":"corpus-368f5152b39b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-d3a4f89c","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"]}}

TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts` calls `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, and `/api/docs/graph`; those endpoints return 404 under static preview but return 200 from the Go server.\n\nBuild a fixture-backed default Playwright runner that boots the Go DDx server on a deterministic local port and points Playwright at it. Do not make tests pass by skipping or deleting them. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:66:{"id":"corpus-47f5902056ab","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add project-scoped route middleware and migrate document/bead/graph routes","description":"## Scope\nAdd project-scoping middleware to the DDx server and migrate all document, bead, and doc-graph HTTP routes to canonical project-scoped paths.\n\n## Context\nThe server currently registers ~50 routes under /api/... without project scoping. Create a projectMiddleware (or helper function) that:\n   - Extracts {project} from the URL path\n   - Resolves it via GetProjectByID() or GetProjectByPath()\n   - Injects the resolved ProjectEntry into request context\n   - Returns 404 if project not found\n2. Register all document, bead, and doc-graph routes under /api/projects/{project}/...\n3. Keep legacy /api/... routes as singleton compatibility aliases that auto-resolve to the single registered project (error if \u003e1 project registered without scoping)\n4. Add test(s) that register two projects, verify scoped routes resolve correctly, and verify legacy routes work with one project\n\n## Files to touch\n- cli/internal/server/server.go — route registration, middleware\n- cli/internal/server/handlers.go (or individual handler files) — extract project from context instead of hardcoding\n- cli/internal/server/server_test.go — new project-scoped routing tests\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n- MCP tools (separate bead)\n- GraphQL schema (already has beadsByProject, workersByProject)\n- Frontend routes","acceptance":"(1) A projectMiddleware or projectResolver extracts {project} from /api/projects/{project}/... routes and injects resolved ProjectEntry into request context. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:162:{"id":"corpus-b32747c53ca5","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Add route-target-route config schema and backend compatibility translation","description":"Implement the config-layer replacement described in FEAT-004, SD-005, and plan-2026-04-10-route-target-first-routing.md. Add routing/route-target_routes config types keyed by requested route-target or canonical target, plus candidate records with upstream-service, optional concrete route-target override, and priority. Preserve direct named-upstream-service config. Load legacy backends/default_backend as deprecated compatibility input, translate them into internal route-target-route records at config-load time, and emit a deprecation warning path that the CLI can surface without breaking existing users. Validation should fail early for unknown upstream-services, empty candidate lists, and unknown route names before agent.Run() starts. Background: DDx expects to hand the embedded runner only route-target intent, not backend labels, so the config surface must make route-target routes the canonical inner-routing abstraction while remaining backward compatible for one migration window.","acceptance":"Config supports routing/route-target_routes plus deprecated backend translation; invalid routes fail during config resolution; legacy backend config maps to the same upstream-service choice as equivalent route-target-route config with a visible deprecation warning path; unit tests cover route parsing, translation, validation, and default-route-target/default-upstream-service precedence.","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:feature","area:lib"],"bead":{"id":"agent-9ae82ce5","title":"Add route-target-route config schema and backend compatibility translation","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Implement the config-layer replacement described in FEAT-004, SD-005, and plan-2026-04-10-route-target-first-routing.md. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:166:{"id":"corpus-b7113543d6ca","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","description":"## Motivation\n\nDDx consumer goal G3: \"the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**.\" Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. When two upstream-services serve the same route-target with different prices, the engine picks by name or score, not cost.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Data route-target\n\nAdd to `internal/routing.Upstream-serviceEntry`:\n\n- `CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` — nil/zero if unknown.\n- `CostSource string` — `\"catalog\" | \"subscription\" | \"unknown\" | \"user-config\"`. Surfaces in decision trace so operators know where the number came from.\n\nPopulate at `buildRoutingInputs` time:\n\n- **Endpoint upstream-services (openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, minimax, qwen, zai, openai):** look up in the existing route-target catalog (`internal/route-targetcatalog`) by resolved route-target ID. When the catalog has a price, use it. When absent, leave zero + `CostSource: \"unknown\"`.\n- **Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini):** compute an **effective** cost as a function of quota utilization:\n  - At 0-70% quota used: effective cost = 0 (fixed-cost subscription, near-zero marginal).\n  - At 70-90%: effective cost = low (encourage spreading load).\n  - At 90-100%: effective cost = high (near-quota demotion).\n  - Exact numbers in the bead acceptance; tunable via `ServiceOptions`.\n  - `CostSource: \"subscription\"`.\n- **`agent` runner (local via endpoints):** cost = electricity estimate from config (off by default — `CostSource: \"unknown\"` when not configured). Operators who want to route-target local cost set `ServiceOptions.LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens`.\n\n### Scoring changes\n\n`internal/routing/score.go`: add cost as a tiebreak dimension **after** eligibility, quota OK, and base score. Order becomes:\n1. Eligible\n2. Quota OK / healthy\n3. Base score (profile policy, cooldowns, perf bias) — unchanged\n4. **Cost (lower is better)** — new\n5. Locality (local cost-class preferred)\n6. Name\n\nFor unknown-cost upstream-services (`CostSource: \"unknown\"`): treat as **neutral** in the cost tiebreak — neither preferred nor demoted, so they don't starve known-cost upstream-services but aren't penalized into last place.\n\n### Unknown-cost policy\n\nA documented rule: when all viable candidates have `CostSource: \"unknown\"`, the cost tiebreak is skipped and locality/name decide. Test covers this.\n\n## Subscription-quota pricing curve\n\nDefault curve (tunable):\n- 0-70% used: effective $/1k tokens = 0\n- 70-80%: 0.1× the pay-per-token rate for the equivalent route-target\n- 80-90%: 0.3× pay-per-token\n- 90-100%: 1.2× pay-per-token (demote below pay-per-token so routing drains the subscription gracefully)","acceptance":"1. `Upstream-serviceEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.\n2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint upstream-services via route-target catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 upstream-services at different prices for the same route-target and asserts correct population.\n3. Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each runner + each curve band.\n4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 upstream-services with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 upstream-services with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).\n5. Unknown-cost upstream-services are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost upstream-services are not starved, unknown-cost upstream-services are not demoted into last place.\n6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent runner with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: \"unknown\"`.\n7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude runner at 92% quota, seed an openrouter upstream-service with equivalent route-target at known cost → openrouter wins.\n8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which upstream-service was picked and why.\n9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).\n10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.","labels":["routing","cost","routing-plan"],"bead":{"id":"agent-0dafc7f0","title":"Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"## Motivation\n\nDDx consumer goal G3: \"the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**.\" Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. Prepend a \"Overview\" (or \"Home\") entry to `pages` in `NavShell.svelte`, routing to `/nodes/\u003cnodeId\u003e/projects/\u003cprojectId\u003e/` (empty path segment after the project id). Use the `LayoutDashboard` icon that's already imported, or pick a dedicated home icon — design call.\n2. Wrap the `DDx` brand in an `\u003ca\u003e` that routes to `/` (node selector) or to the current project's home if one is selected. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:206:{"id":"corpus-e0489fc7c76e","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"test(service): tests calling agent.New() leak user config — break verify against base revisions","description":"Several service_*_test.go tests call agent.New(...) with default options. agent.New defaults to loading the user's \\$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/agent/config.yaml. Any upstream-service type added to the user's config that the agent module under test doesn't recognize causes those tests to fail with 'unknown upstream-service type'.\n\nToday's beadbench preflight run (agent-beadbench-preflight against agent-openrouter-qwen36plus, 2026-04-28) hit this: the bead executes correctly through the route-target loop, but the verify gate runs 'go test ./...' against the bead's verify-worktree at base_rev 8925d4b (pre-lucebox). That older code rejects 'type: lucebox' / 'type: vllm' from the user's live config.\n\nFailing tests captured (sample):\n\n  service_upstream-services_test.go:680 TestNewWaitsBrieflyForInvalidQuotaRefresh\n  service_upstream-services_test.go:721 TestNewStartupQuotaRefreshContinuesAfterTimeout\n  service_upstream-services_test.go:765 TestPrimaryQuotaRefreshWorkerRefreshesOnTimer\n  service_validation_test.go:15 TestExecuteRejectsUnsupportedSubprocessRoute-targetBeforeRun\n  service_validation_test.go:43 TestExecuteRejectsUnsupportedSubprocessReasoningBeforeRun\n  service_runner_dispatch_test.go:41,90,119 TestExecute_Dispatches*\n  service_profiles_test.go:13,61,112 TestServiceProfiles_*\n\nAll same root cause: agent.New() loads the live user config without test isolation.\n\n## Fix paths\n\n1. Tests that don't actually need the user's config should pass an explicit ServiceConfig stub (no upstream-services, or a known shape).\n2. Or, set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp dir in test setup so agent.New() loads an empty config.\n3. Or, add an option to agent.New that skips XDG lookup.\n\nPick whichever is most consistent with how other test-isolation patterns work in this repo.\n\n## Why P2\n\nDoesn't block normal CI (CI clean container has no user config). Does affect any verify-gate run on a base revision predating a upstream-service-type addition — a bead correctness signal we care about during parity measurements.","acceptance":"1. service_*_test.go tests calling agent.New() with default options either set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a t.TempDir() or pass an explicit ServiceConfig that doesn't depend on the user's live config.\n2. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:211:{"id":"corpus-e3d55d44b044","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns.
