./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:231:{"id":"corpus-f744aa57e91b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple execute-loop workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-55f05e07","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple execute-loop workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e"}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:238:{"id":"corpus-011259e83fe4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"execute-loop\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","bug","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-05b4cc9d","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:259:{"id":"corpus-ef0f9a2702f5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Runner: claude / route-target: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of route-target time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple runneres (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one upstream-service's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The runner should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the runner records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","labels":["area:agent","area:runner","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.\n    \u003c/description\u003e\n    \u003cacceptance\u003e\n- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles\n- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)\n- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order\n- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)\n- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep\n- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)\n- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths\n- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)\n- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly\n- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n    \u003c/acceptance\u003e\n    \u003clabels\u003ehelix, area:auth, spec:ADR-018, phase:middleware\u003c/labels\u003e\n    \u003cmetadata parent=\"axon-b170a173\" base-rev=\"HEAD\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual\"/\u003e\n  \u003c/bead\u003e\n\n  \u003cgoverning\u003e\n    \u003cref id=\"ADR-018\" path=\"docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-018-tenant-user-credential-model.md\"\u003eSections 4 (Credentials) and 4 Verification Order — normative for everything in this bead\u003c/ref\u003e\n    \u003cref id=\"FEAT-012\" path=\"docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-authorization.md\"\u003eCredentials (JWT, V5) and Identity (Authentication) sections\u003c/ref\u003e\n  \u003c/governing\u003e\n\n  \u003cinstructions\u003e\nYou are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. [axon-dccbdf73]'`. SQLite connection.\\n15\\t///\\n16\\t/// This is idempotent — running it multiple times on the same database\\n17\\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\\n18\\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: \u0026rusqlite::Connection) -\u003e Result\u003c(), String\u003e {\\n19\\t    // Create tenants table.\\n20\\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\\n21\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n22\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\\n23\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n24\\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\\n25\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n26\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n27\\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\\n28\\t        )\\\",\\n29\\t    )\\n30\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenants table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n31\\t\\n32\\t    // Create users table.\\n33\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n34\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\\n35\\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\\n36\\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n37\\t            email              TEXT,\\n38\\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n39\\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\\n40\\t        )\\\",\\n41\\t    )\\n42\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n43\\t\\n44\\t    // Create user_identities table.\\n45\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\\n46\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n47\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\\n48\\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\\n49\\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n50\\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n51\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n52\\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\\n53\\t        )\\\",\\n54\\t    )\\n55\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"user_identities table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n56\\t\\n57\\t    // Create tenant_users table.\\n58\\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\\n59\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n60\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\\n61\\t            tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n62\\t            user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\\n63\\t            role         TEXT NOT NULL,\\n64\\t            added_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n65\\t            PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\\n66\\t        )\\\",\\n67\\t    )\\n68\\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\\\"tenant_users table: {e}\\\"))?;\\n69\\t\\n70\\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\\n71\\t    conn.execute_batch(\\n72\\t        \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\\n73\\t            tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\\n74\\t            database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\\n75\\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n76\\t            PRIMARY KEY Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n           \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\\\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\\n                    \\\"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\\\"\\n url(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n            \u0026self.url\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    struct TestStore {\\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\\n        _database: TestDatabase,\\n }\\n\\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\\n        fn deref_mut(\u0026mut self) -\u003e \u0026mut Self::Target {\\n            \u0026mut self.adapter\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(|| {\\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \\\"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003cTestDatabase\u003e {\\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n    fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. ./docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:59:| D5 | **Wire transport**: GraphQL only. | All DDx↔Axon traffic is GraphQL — queries and mutations over HTTP, subscriptions over WebSocket. No gRPC, no REST, no MCP from the DDx side. The Go client is generated by `genqlient` from a pinned schema-introspection snapshot. Rationale: Axon's GraphQL surface already covers entity CRUD, links, traversal, transactions, schema, and subscriptions; DDx is already a GraphQL server internally (`cli/internal/server/graphql_*.go`), so DDx↔Axon is wire-symmetric; subscriptions unlock live UI updates for `ddx work` (Story 8) without polling; no protoc/codegen toolchain enters the DDx build. The per-call wire-overhead advantage of gRPC is immeasurable at DDx scale (low thousands of beads); if batch-transaction throughput later becomes painful for one specific path, gRPC can be added there without revisiting this decision. |

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:238:{"id":"corpus-011259e83fe4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","description":"## Observed

User clicks "Drain queue" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/<id>/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.

Verified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.

```
$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json
{
  "id": "worker-20260422T205846-f23f",
  "kind": "execute-loop",
  "state": "running",
  "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",
  ...
}
```

So the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.

## Root cause

`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:

```go
if projectID != "" && rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {
    continue
}
```

- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.
- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.

These are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.

The global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `""` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.

Note: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.

## Proposed direction

Two options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.

### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site

```go
expectedPath := ""
if projectID != "" {
    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)
    if !ok {
        return nil // unknown project id → no workers
    }
    expectedPath = proj.Path
}
...
if expectedPath != "" && rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {
    continue
}
```

### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record

Add `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).

Recommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.

## Scope note

This is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.

## Out of scope

- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).
- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.

2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**
   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).
   - Start one worker for each project.
   - `workersByProject(projectID: <id-1>)` returns exactly worker 1.
   - `workersByProject(projectID: <id-2>)` returns exactly worker 2.
   - `workers` (global) returns both.
   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.

3. **Regression test — backward compat.**
   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.

4. **End-to-end validation.**
   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.
   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.

5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**
   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.
   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.
   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.

6. **Cross-reference.**
   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. 				}
			}
		}
	`;

	interface AgentMetricsRow {
		key: string;
		attempts: number;
		successes: number;
		successRate: number;
		meanDurationMs: number;
		p50DurationMs: number;
		p95DurationMs: number;
		meanCostUsd: number;
		effectiveCostPerSuccessUsd: number | null;
		meanInputTokens: number;
		meanOutputTokens: number;

					tokensUsedLast24h
					requestsLastHour
					requestsLast24h
				}
				quota {

					ceilingWindowSeconds
					remaining
					resetAt
				}
				sparkline
			}
			harnessStatuses {

				baseURL
				model
				status
				reachable
				detail
				modelCount
				isDefault
				cooldownUntil
				lastCheckedAt
				defaultForProfile
				usage {

				}

					remaining
					resetAt
				}
				sparkline
			}
		}
	`;

	const PROVIDER_MODELS_QUERY = gql`

			providerModels(name: $name, kind: $kind) {

				kind

				fromCache
				models {
					id
					contextLength
					available
				}
			}
		}
	`;

	const REFRESH_PROVIDER_MODELS_MUTATION = gql`
		mutation RefreshProviderModels($name: String!, $kind: ProviderKind!) {
			refreshProviderModels(name: $name, kind: $kind) {
				name
				kind
				baseURL
				fetchedAt

					contextLength
					available
				}

		}
	`;

	const DEFAULT_ROUTE_QUERY = gql`

data.text: I’ve confirmed the provider detail route currently only has trend data; the worker-count part is more likely to belong to the availability list rows. I’m checking the existing e2e coverage and any server read-model helpers for a preexisting “recent worker” concept before I edit the API shape.

I’ve confirmed the provider trend resolver already uses the session index, which is probably the right source for a recent worker count. I’m checking the schema and current provider tests to see whether the count should be added to the API read model or rendered by reusing existing session usage data on the client.

data.text: I’ve got a plausible path now: compute the count from the existing session index, surface it as a `recentWorkerCount` field on the GraphQL `ProviderStatus` read model, and then render that badge on the availability list next to each provider row. I’m checking the generated model shape so I can patch the schema and resolver without breaking codegen invariants.

The GraphQL schema is checked in, so a new field will require touching the schema plus the generated Go model/field dispatch. I’m checking whether this repo has a local regenerate command; if not, I’ll patch the small generated surface directly and keep the change narrow.

data.text: The e2e run is blocked by an already-running `ddx server` on the machine, which the Playwright webServer refuses to replace. I’m checking the server status so I can decide whether to stop that stray instance or take the safer fallback path.

data.output: 2986169     618 /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx server --port 7743

data.text: The clean path is to stop the background `ddx-server.service`, run the Playwright slice against the temp server built from this worktree, then restore the service afterward. I’m doing that now.

I’ve got the code changes in place and the remaining work is to finish the frontend verification, then I’ll package the bead cleanly without touching unrelated tracker noise. I’m polling the Playwright run now and will fix anything it surfaces before I commit.

The frontend suite is mostly green; I’m down to a few failing worker tests now, which is usually a sign one fixture or selector needs to be adjusted rather than a broad regression. I’m pulling the failure details so I can patch the exact breakage and rerun only the affected specs.

data.text: The failures are narrow and mostly test expectations, not product logic: one strict heading selector, one hard-coded node id, and one link target that now reflects the real project-runs route. I’m inspecting the workers spec around those assertions and the page code so I can align the tests with the current UI instead of papering over the symptom.

data.output:
<script lang="ts">
	import type { LayoutData } from './$types';
	import type { Snippet } from 'svelte';
	import { goto, invalidateAll } from '$app/navigation';
	import { page } from '$app/stores';
	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
	import { subscribeWorkerProgress } from '$lib/gql/subscriptions';
	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';

	let { data, children }: { data: LayoutData; children: Snippet } = $props();

	const START_WORKER_MUTATION = gql`
		mutation StartWorker($input: StartWorkerInput!) {
			startWorker(input: $input) {
				id
				state
				kind
			}
		}
	`;

	const STOP_WORKER_MUTATION = gql`
		mutation StopWorker($id: ID!) {
			stopWorker(id: $id) {
				id
				state
				kind
			}
		}
	`;

	// + Add worker dispatches a default-spec drain worker (ddx-b6cf025c). The
	// server honours .ddx/config.yaml workers.default_spec + workers.max_count.
	const ADD_WORKER_MUTATION = gql`
		mutation AddDrainWorker($projectId: String!) {
			workerDispatch(kind: "execute-loop", projectId: $projectId) {
				id
				state
				kind
			}
		}
	`;

	// Live phase overrides from workerProgress subscription (workerID -> phase)
	let livePhaseOverrides = $state<Map<string, string>>(new Map());
	let showStartForm = $state(false);
	let starting = $state(false);
	let stoppingId = $state<string | null>(null);
	let actionError = $state<string | null>(null);
	let harness = $state('');
	let profile = $state('smart');
	let effort = $state('medium');
	let labelFilter = $state('');
	let adding = $state(false);
	let removing = $state(false);

	// Drain workers: count of running execute-loop workers.
	const runningDrainCount = $derived(
		data.workers.edges.filter(
			(e) => e.node.state === 'running' && e.node.kind === 'execute-loop'
		).length
	);

	// workers.max_count safety rail (ddx-b6cf025c). Null = no cap configured.
	const maxCount = $derived(data.maxCount);
	const atMaxCount = $derived(
		typeof maxCount === 'number' && maxCount >= 0 && runningDrainCount >= maxCount
	);
	const addDisabled = $derived(adding || atMaxCount);
	const addTooltip = $derived(
		atMaxCount ? 'at workers.max_count limit' : 'Add a general-purpose drain worker'
	);

	// Subscribe to progress events for all running workers
	$effect(() => {
		const runningIds = data.workers.edges
			.filter((e) => e.node.state === 'running')
			.map((e) => e.node.id);

		livePhaseOverrides = new Map();

		const disposes = runningIds.map((workerID) =>
			subscribeWorkerProgress(workerID, (evt) => {
				const next = new Map(livePhaseOverrides);
				next.set(evt.workerID, evt.phase);
				livePhaseOverrides = next;
			})
		);

		return () => disposes.forEach((d) => d());
	});

	// The currently open worker (from child route params)
	let activeWorker = $derived(($page.params as Record<string, string>)['workerId'] ?? null);

	function openWorker(workerId: string) {
		const p = $page.params as Record<string, string>;
		goto(`/nodes/${p['nodeId']}/projects/${p['projectId']}/workers/${workerId}`);
	}

	function sessionsHref(): string {
		const p = $page.params as Record<string, string>;
		return `/nodes/${p['nodeId']}/projects/${p['projectId']}/runs?layer=run`;
	}

	function providerHref(name: string): string {
		const p = $page.params as Record<string, string>;
		return `/nodes/${p['nodeId']}/providers/${encodeURIComponent(name)}`;
	}

	function errorText(err: unknown): string {
		return err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Worker action failed.';
	}

	async function startWorker() {
		actionError = null;
		if (!profile.trim() || !effort.trim()) {
			actionError = 'Profile and effort are required.';
			return;
		}
		starting = true;
		try {
			const client = createClient(fetch);
			await client.request(START_WORKER_MUTATION, {
				input: {
					projectId: data.projectId,
					harness: harness.trim() || null,
					profile: profile.trim(),
					effort: effort.trim(),
					labelFilter: labelFilter.trim() || null
				}
			});
			showStartForm = false;
			harness = '';
			labelFilter = '';
			await invalidateAll();
		} catch (err) {
			actionError = errorText(err);
		} finally {
			starting = false;
		}
	}

	async function addDrainWorker() {
		actionError = null;
		adding = true;
		try {
			const client = createClient(fetch);
			await client.request(ADD_WORKER_MUTATION, { projectId: data.projectId });
			await invalidateAll();
		} catch (err) {
			actionError = errorText(err);
		} finally {
			adding = false;
		}
	}

	async function removeDrainWorker() {
		actionError = null;
		// Find the oldest running execute-loop worker (AC #4: "stops the oldest-
		// running drain worker"). data.workers is sorted newest-first, so the
		// last matching edge is oldest.
		const runningDrain = data.workers.edges
			.filter((e) => e.node.state === 'running' && e.node.kind === 'execute-loop')
			.map((e) => e.node);
		const target = runningDrain[runningDrain.length - 1];
		if (!target) return;
		if (!window.confirm(`Stop worker ${target.id}?`)) return;
		removing = true;
		try {
			const client = createClient(fetch);
			await client.request(STOP_WORKER_MUTATION, { id: target.id });
			await invalidateAll();
		} catch (err) {
			actionError = errorText(err);
		} finally {
			removing = false;
		}
	}

	async function stopWorker(event: MouseEvent, workerId: string) {
		event.stopPropagation();
		actionError = null;
		if (!window.confirm(`Stop worker ${workerId}?`)) return;
		stoppingId = workerId;
		try {
			const client = createClient(fetch);
			await client.request(STOP_WORKER_MUTATION, { id: workerId });
			await invalidateAll();
		} catch (err) {
			actionError = errorText(err);
		} finally {
			stoppingId = null;
		}
	}

	function stateClass(state: string): string {
		switch (state) {
			case 'running':
				return 'text-status-running';
			case 'idle':
				return 'text-status-open';
			case 'stopped':
				return 'text-fg-muted dark:text-dark-fg-muted';
			case 'error':
				return 'text-status-failed';
			default:
				return 'text-fg-muted dark:text-dark-fg-muted';
		}
	}

	// ADR-022 step 5b: freshness badge classes for reported (derived-view)
	// workers. The state strings come from the server (connected | stale |
	// disconnected) — see ReportedWorker.state in schema.graphql.
	function freshnessBadgeClass(state: string): string {
		switch (state) {
			case 'connected':
				return 'border-status-running bg-status-running/10 text-status-running';
			case 'stale':
				return 'border-status-open bg-status-open/10 text-status-open';
			case 'disconnected':
				return 'border-status-failed bg-status-failed/10 text-status-failed';
			default:
				return 'border-border-line bg-bg-surface text-fg-muted dark:border-dark-border-line dark:bg-dark-bg-surface dark:text-dark-fg-muted';
		}
	}

	function formatTimestamp(iso: string): string {
		try {
			return new Date(iso).toLocaleString();
		} catch {
			return iso;
		}
	}

	// Group reported workers by project root so duplicates (same project, two
	// worker_ids) render distinctly per ADR-022 §worker-server-interface
	// (multiple workers per project root is expected and visible).
	const reportedByProject = $derived.by(() => {
		const groups = new Map<string, typeof data.reportedWorkers>();

data.text: I found the root causes: the workers page has a second heading that makes the generic `Workers` locator strict, the route under test now emits `/runs?layer=run`, and the provider-link assertion should key off the actual mock node id from the fixture instead of a stale literal. I’m patching those expectations in the Playwright spec only; the product code already matches the current routes.

data.text: I’m making a tight test-only patch now: exact-match the page heading, point the provider-link check at the fixture node id, and assert the actual recent-sessions route the layout generates. That keeps the new feature covered without leaving brittle expectations behind.

The worker-spec rerun is building cleanly. If this passes, the only remaining steps are to restore the local ddx service I stopped for the test harness, stage the scoped files, and make the single required commit.

data.text: The last mismatch is the content check after navigation. The destination is now the live runs page, so I’m swapping the old sessions-copy assertion for the runs-page heading that the app actually renders.

data.output:
<script lang="ts">
	import type { PageData } from './$types'
	import { page } from '$app/stores'
	import { goto } from '$app/navigation'
	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
	import { PROJECT_RUNS_QUERY, PAGE_SIZE } from './+page'
	import type { RunEdge, RunNode, RunConnection, PageInfo } from './+page'
	import { RunRowDetail } from '$lib/runDetail'
	import ConfirmDialog from '$lib/components/ConfirmDialog.svelte'
	import FilterChip from '$lib/components/FilterChip.svelte'
	import { RUN_REQUEUE_MUTATION, WORKER_DISPATCH_MUTATION } from '$lib/gql/feat008'

	// Threshold above which the runs table opts into browser-native row
	// windowing via `content-visibility: auto`. Keeps DOM semantics, table
	// layout, row expansion, and any future live worker phase subscriptions
	// intact — the browser simply skips rendering off-screen rows.
	const VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD = 1000
	const VIRTUAL_ROW_HEIGHT_PX = 49

	let { data }: { data: PageData } = $props()

	let appendedEdges = $state<RunEdge[]>([])
	let appendedPageInfo = $state<PageInfo | null>(null)
	let loadingMore = $state(false)
	let harnessInput = $state(data.activeHarness ?? '')
	let expanded = $state<Set<string>>(new Set())

	type ActionMode = 'requeue' | 'startWorker'
	let actionDialogOpen = $state(false)
	let actionMode = $state<ActionMode>('requeue')
	let actionRun = $state<RunNode | null>(null)
	let actionIdempotencyKey = $state('')
	let actionLayerOverride = $state('')
	let actionWorkerArgs = $state('')
	let actionAlert = $state('')
	let actionResultMessage = $state('')

	function newIdempotencyKey(): string {
		if (typeof crypto !== 'undefined' && typeof crypto.randomUUID === 'function') {
			return crypto.randomUUID()
		}
		return `requeue-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)}`
	}

	function openRequeueDialog(run: RunNode) {
		actionMode = 'requeue'
		actionRun = run
		actionIdempotencyKey = newIdempotencyKey()
		actionLayerOverride = run.layer
		actionAlert = ''
		actionResultMessage = ''
		actionDialogOpen = true
	}

	function openStartWorkerDialog(run: RunNode) {
		actionMode = 'startWorker'
		actionRun = run
		actionAlert = ''
		actionResultMessage = ''
		// Prefill the worker args with the original drain's queueInputs JSON.
		// Pretty-print so the operator can review/edit before dispatching.
		if (run.queueInputs) {
			try {
				actionWorkerArgs = JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(run.queueInputs), null, 2)
			} catch {
				actionWorkerArgs = run.queueInputs
			}
		} else {
			actionWorkerArgs = JSON.stringify(
				{ source: 'runs-start-worker', sourceRunId: run.id },
				null,
				2
			)
		}
		actionDialogOpen = true
	}

	async function confirmAction() {
		if (!actionRun) return
		const client = createClient()
		try {
			if (actionMode === 'requeue') {
				if (!actionIdempotencyKey.trim()) {
					actionAlert = 'Idempotency key is required.'
					throw new Error('missing idempotency key')
				}
				const layer = actionLayerOverride.trim()
				const result = await client.request<{
					runRequeue: { bead: { id: string }; deduplicated: boolean }
				}>(RUN_REQUEUE_MUTATION, {
					input: {
						runId: actionRun.id,
						idempotencyKey: actionIdempotencyKey,
						layer: layer ? layer : null
					}
				})
				actionResultMessage = result.runRequeue.deduplicated
					? `Bead ${result.runRequeue.bead.id} already requeued (deduplicated).`
					: `Bead ${result.runRequeue.bead.id} requeued.`
			} else {
				// startWorker: validate JSON before sending so the operator can fix it inline.
				let argsString = actionWorkerArgs.trim()
				if (argsString) {
					try {
						JSON.parse(argsString)
					} catch (err) {
						actionAlert = `Worker args must be valid JSON: ${errorText(err)}`
						throw err
					}
				} else {
					argsString = '{}'
				}
				const projectId = actionRun.projectID ?? data.projectId
				const result = await client.request<{
					workerDispatch: { id: string; state: string; kind: string }
				}>(WORKER_DISPATCH_MUTATION, {
					kind: 'execute-loop',
					projectId,
					args: argsString
				})
				actionResultMessage = `Worker ${result.workerDispatch.id} started (${result.workerDispatch.state}).`
			}
		} catch (err) {
			if (!actionAlert) actionAlert = errorText(err)
			throw err
		}
	}

	function errorText(err: unknown): string {
		if (err instanceof Error) return err.message
		if (typeof err === 'string') return err
		return 'Unknown error.'
	}

	function toggleExpand(runId: string) {
		const next = new Set(expanded)
		if (next.has(runId)) next.delete(runId)
		else next.add(runId)
		expanded = next
	}
